title: Spelling
body:
This PR corrects misspellings identified by the [check-spelling action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/check-spelling).

The misspellings have been reported at https://github.com/jsoref/podman/commit/04624679014630f9d7d486be6262faac1fcacc39#commitcomment-45284288

The action reports that the changes in this PR would make it happy: https://github.com/jsoref/podman/commit/83328c1aa180d27ccc45ae030679eca30274f212

Note: this PR does not include the action. If you're interested in running a spell check on every PR and push, that can be offered separately.

---
I understand that changes.txt is generated from commit messages, but commit messages have typos, which make them fairly misleading. It's trivial for me to drop changes for this file if requested (or any other file(s)).

comments.body: Restarted one test failure, [int podman fedora32 root container](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5043977965535232/html/int-podman-fedora-32-root-container.log.html#t--podman-pod-rm-removes-a-pod-with-a-container--1), which failed with "error removing pod". That's not a known flake, but I also don't see any way this PR could've caused it.

Fwiw, you're using both `rootfull` and `rootful`. I guessed wrong based on what felt like dominance. I'll swap them out (it doesn't hurt me, although it's much easier to do before I squash).

If you could give me a heuristic for which things might be ABI breaks, I'd be happy to split them off.

I'm reverting to the unsquashed version for the time being. People can identify commits they'd like me to drop and I'll drop them.

When people are actually ready for this to be merged, can someone authoritatively instruct me to squash? (or you of course have my permission to squash them)
thansk for contribute, you should rebase all commits to one commit at first
First squash commits and do not change changelogs.
Thanks @jsoref this is a really nice cleanup, and sadly not stuff found by codecleanup.

>I think we should stick to the name "rootfull" rather then "rootful"

I need to make one last plea: English language rules allow for the use of ["-ful" as a suffix](https://www.etymonline.com/word/-ful), never ["-full"](https://www.etymonline.com/search?q=-full). All instances of "rootfull" are incorrect and jarring; changing existing "rootful" to "rootfull" is making a bad situation much worse. The links above are the result of a quick web search; I'm sorry I don't know how to find definitive references, this is like the how's-the-water fish anecdote: grammar is such a basic deep-down component of my being that I don't really know how/where to find references to fundamental rules. If you need further support on this, please find any language expert you trust and ask them.

>I'm reverting to the unsquashed version for the time being. 

Since this PR may nee further iteration, I'm tentatively OK with @jsoref pushing whatever they prefer during review time, as long as the final version is squashed.
Ok if the proper english is ful, then we can go with that. but we need to make sure it is changed everywhere.
No this is fine, you just need to squash your commits.  When I say everywhere I am talking about mentions outside of the podman repo.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
